title: MD
biography: Helen Brooke Taussig was an American cardiologist who founded the field of pediatric cardiology. She conceptualized the Blalock-Taussig shunt procedure that saved thousands of 'blue babies' with cyanotic heart defects, and later was instrumental in preventing the thalidomide disaster in the United States.
aiSummary: Helen Brooke Taussig was the founder of pediatric cardiology who conceptualized the Blalock-Taussig shunt for blue baby syndrome. Also prevented thalidomide disaster in the US. Based in Baltimore, United States.
education: Johns Hopkins University School of Medicine

timeline.title: First Blalock-Taussig shunt operation
timeline.description: Her concept was performed by Alfred Blalock on Eileen Saxon, saving the first 'blue baby'

timeline.title: Published 'Congenital Malformations of the Heart'
timeline.description: Wrote the definitive textbook on congenital heart disease

timeline.title: Prevented US thalidomide approval
timeline.description: Traveled to Germany to investigate thalidomide's effects and testified to Congress, preventing its US sale

timeline.title: Presidential Medal of Freedom
timeline.description: Awarded the highest civilian honor for her contributions to medicine
